commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram isolated polling shutdown and gateway hot-reload channel stops so reload-triggered Telegram polling exits can recover, then adds regression tests and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for a high-confidence source-level path: the linked issue gives concrete hot-reload steps/logs, and current main still has uncaught isolated worker rejection plus manual hot-reload stop semantics. I did not run a before-fix live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair lane is needed because the PR is already a focused fix with live proof and no blocking findings; maintainers should proceed through normal review/CI.

target.title: Fix Telegram hot reload polling restarts
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat gateway channel hot reload stops as restart/recovery stops, not manual user stops.
- Make Telegram isolated ingress worker shutdown observable and restart recoverable worker exits without retrying fatal Telegram API errors.
- Add regression coverage for reload stop semantics and isolated ingress worker exit handling.

Fixes #83008

## Verification
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-worker.runtime.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts && git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- AWS Crabbox `run_e1faddefd8e4` (`aws`, `cbx_092d504c124b` / `swift-lobster`): live Telegram reload proof passed.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram config hot reload could wedge isolated polling after a restart stop was treated as a manual stop and the ingress worker exited with code 1.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox direct provider with a live Convex-leased `telegram` credential and a real Telegram group/bots; a mock OpenAI Responses server delayed the in-flight model turn.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: AWS Crabbox `run_e1faddefd8e4` forced `channels.telegram.replyToMode` hot reload (`first` -> `off`) while a delayed `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_RELOAD_INFLIGHT` Telegram turn was active, then sent a post-reload `/status`.

Evidence after fix: `configHotReloadApplied: true`, `isolatedIngressStarts: 2`, in-flight reply observed, post-reload `/status` reply observed, `stopTimeoutLogCount: 0`, `workerExitCode1LogCount: 0`.

Observed result after fix: Telegram isolated polling restarted after hot reload and continued replying.

What was not tested: full repository CI before opening this PR; that will run on the PR.
target.author: joshavant
